ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a mushroom sandwich combo meal with cookie. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR (Sandwiches) is the closest parent category. No specific code exists for a mushroom sandwich cookie combo meal.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard combo meal consisting of a mushroom sub sandwich (approximately 280 g) and one cookie (approximately 70 g), totalling roughly 350 g as served at Subway Singapore or equivalent outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Veggie Delite sandwich and Subway chocolate chip cookie — USDA Branded Food / Subway nutritional data adapted for mushroom variant; supplemented with USDA FoodData Central mushroom and cookie component data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods and SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for 'Mushroom Delite Cookie Meal' as a named product. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis: (1) Subway Veggie Delite 6-inch sub adapted with mushroom filling (USDA Branded Food FDC ID 2103706 and related entries), (2) Subway chocolate chip cookie (USDA Branded Food FDC ID 2103748). Values are blended per 100 g of the combined 350 g serving. All per-100g values are derived from this weighted component estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — white bread and mixed sandwich analogues; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Mushroom Delite Cookie Meal. GI of 56 is estimated as a weighted average of: (1) white sub bread GI ~70 (University of Sydney GI Database, white bread reference), (2) mushroom and vegetable filling GI ~15 (low-GI vegetables, University of Sydney GI Database), (3) chocolate chip cookie GI ~41 (Atkinson et al. 2021, Diabetes Care). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution across the 350 g serving. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (56 × 134.75) / 100 ≈ 75.3.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~390 mg per 100 g; ~1365 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal due to its high sodium content of approximately 1365 mg per serving, which represents over 60% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; requesting reduced-sodium sauces and omitting pickles can partially mitigate sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~56) and high carbohydrate load per serving (~135 g carbohydrates)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this combo meal as its medium GI of approximately 56 and high total carbohydrate load per serving (~135 g) can cause a significant postprandial glucose rise; portion reduction, choosing wholegrain bread where available, and omitting the cookie can substantially lower the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~2.4 g per 100 g) and high sodium (~390 mg per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the combination of moderate saturated fat and high sodium in this meal warrants caution; selecting low-fat sauce options, increasing vegetable toppings, and avoiding the cookie side can improve the cardiovascular risk profile of this meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Mushroom Delite Cookie Meal is a combo meal served at Subway Singapore and select mix-and-match outlets, consisting of a mushroom-filled sub sandwich paired with a cookie as a side item. The sandwich component features sautéed or marinated mushrooms as the primary filling, layered with fresh vegetables such as lettuce, tomato, and onion on a soft white or Italian sub roll, finished with a choice of sauce or dressing. The cookie component is typically a soft-baked chocolate chip or oatmeal raisin cookie. The overall flavour profile is savoury and mildly umami from the mushrooms, balanced by the sweetness of the cookie. Nutritionally, this meal is carbohydrate-dense, providing approximately 248 kcal per 100 g and around 870 kcal per full 350 g serving. It is a moderate source of protein (~7.2 g per 100 g) and contains meaningful dietary fibre (~2.1 g per 100 g) from the vegetable and bread components. The sodium content is notably high (~390 mg per 100 g), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 56 (medium), driven primarily by the refined white bread base and the cookie, making portion control important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The meal is suitable for vegetarians and is generally considered halal. It is not suitable for those with gluten intolerance due to the wheat-based bread and cookie. This meal is commonly ordered as a quick-service lunch or dinner option in Singapore's fast-casual dining segment.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for 'Mushroom Delite Cookie Meal' was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any other approved primary source as a named composite product. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA Branded Food data for Subway Veggie Delite sub and Subway cookie components, adapted for the mushroom filling variant. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for white bread, vegetables, and cookie. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Vitamin B12 is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported for this vegetarian sandwich product in the component sources used; mushroom-based vegetarian meals typically contain negligible B12 unless fortified. 2. Vitamin D is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food component sources used for this estimation. 3. Vitamin E is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food component sources used for this estimation. 4. Selenium is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food component sources used for this estimation. 5. All per-100g values are derived from a weighted component estimate across a 350 g serving (approximately 280 g mushroom sub + 70 g cookie); values should be verified against official Subway Singapore nutritional disclosure if and when available. 6. The food_group has been corrected from the input value 'Vegetables' to 'Mixed' as this is a composite combo meal containing bread, vegetables, and a cookie. 7. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI 56 × carbohydrate per serving 134.75 g) / 100 = 75.3. 8. The cuisine tag 'Sandwich' from the input is preserved; the broader cuisine classification is Western / Sandwich consistent with Subway's brand positioning. 9. Multilingual names are provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this food is served in Singapore. 10. No zero-value core macros are present; zero-macro justification rule is not triggered.
